title: Family of Mass. worker who died after getting trapped in freezer gets nearly $13 million in settlement
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts worker's family receives nearly $13 million settlement after wrongful death in freezer incident.
The case focused on a freezer's faulty interior door handle, implicating the company and manufacturer.
Legal representation involved extensive investigation, including expert testimonies on freezer defects and workplace safety.

title: Clover plans to reopen some locations after sudden closure, thanks to an anonymous investor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Clover Food Lab will reopen its Cambridge and Boston locations on June 9 after a deal with an anonymous investor.
The restaurant chain initially announced closures due to inflation and financial struggles, impacting its 11 locations and meal delivery service.
Clover will focus on a reduced footprint and continue sourcing ingredients locally, aligning with its core community-focused mission.

title: Abreu has a two-run homer and 3 RBIs, Tolle pitches 6 scoreless innings and Red Sox beat Orioles 8-1
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit a two-run homer and had three RBIs as the Red Sox defeated the Orioles 8-1.
Payton Tolle pitched six scoreless innings, allowing seven hits and striking out five, lowering his ERA to 2.28.
Mickey Gasper's two-run triple contributed to Boston's five-run fifth inning; every Red Sox player had at least one hit.

title: MassDems accuse GOP candidates of intentionally submitting false signatures to make primary ballot
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: MassDems accused GOP candidates Mike Walsh and Anne Manning-Martin of submitting fraudulent signatures for the Massachusetts primary ballot.
Objections were filed by MassDems with the State Ballot Law Commission, citing signatures not signed in person or by non-registered voters.
If proven fraudulent, Walsh and Manning-Martin could be disqualified from the primary for lacking the required 10,000 valid signatures.

title: Isiah Kiner-Falefa, Chad Tracy elaborate on cryptic comments on Red Sox’ Fenway struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Isiah Kiner-Falefa cited distractions at Fenway Park as one reason for the Red Sox's struggles at home.
Chad Tracy agreed with Kiner-Falefa, highlighting increased media and personnel presence at Fenway Park.
Despite home challenges, the Red Sox have a 16-14 record on the road this season.

title: Teen found dismembered in Chelsea parking lot is identified 26 years later
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tiffany Bradley, a teen found dismembered in Chelsea in 2000, has been identified 26 years later using DNA advances.
Eugene McCollom, who confessed to Bradley's murder, remains incarcerated despite her identity being unknown for decades.
Bradley was identified through DNA from her brother; she was trafficked from Pennsylvania and murdered shortly after arriving in Boston.

title: Olney: Red Sox could take on a ‘bad contract’ in trade for right-handed bat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox rank 27th in runs and 29th in home runs as of June, sitting at the bottom of the AL East.
Boston is actively seeking a right-handed batter, willing to absorb a substantial contract to boost offensive performance.
Potential trade targets include Willy Adames, Matt Chapman, Bo Bichette, and Fernando Tatis Jr., despite possible risks.

title: Here’s how to score one of the new commemorative CharlieCards
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA has released special edition CharlieCards featuring landmarks like Boston Public Library and USS Constitution.
Decorative CharlieCards are currently available at Park Street, Downtown Crossing, and Forest Hills stations.
More than a dozen T stations will distribute the collectible cards within the next week, availability is sporadic.

title: Jaylen Brown’s Fort Point penthouse is back on the market for $4.9m
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Jaylen Brown re-lists his Fort Point penthouse for $4.9 million, now including an adjacent studio residence.
The 3,500-square-foot property has an open floor plan, exposed brick, and a 400-square-foot roof deck.
Originally constructed in 1910, the condo is located in a building with a popular restaurant, Mooo…. Seaport.

title: Patriots WR Kayshon Boutte ‘interested’ in a trade, NFL insider says
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots WR Kayshon Boutte is interested in a trade, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.
Boutte, a 2023 sixth-round pick, missed Patriots' first two OTAs and seeks a trade amid a crowded receiver room.
Boutte recorded 551 receiving yards and 6 touchdowns last season, playing 55% of Patriots' offensive snaps.
